daniel.label: DANIEL × PRIYA · ON TRACK · weekly check-in call
daniel.framing: Daniel Okafor, 38, ex-warehouse, ON-TRACK. Confident, ready. Priya does the Tuesday check-in.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Hi Daniel, it's Priya. Just my Tuesday check-in, are you alright to talk for two minutes?

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Yeah, fine. Just on my way back from the gym.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Brilliant. Right, your numbers are properly strong. Top quartile in your cohort. CV is signed off, you've saved nine vacancies, and the Level 2 course is locked in for the first of July.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Yeah it's been alright, actually. I feel like I'm getting somewhere.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: I want to push you a little. Sundown Logistics is your match-of-the-week, but there's a Senior Warehouse Operative slot at Bow Distribution. Three grand more, same commute. Want me to send it across?

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Senior, though? I haven't done senior before.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: You have, you just didn't call it that. Two years team-leading at Hackney was supervising six people. I'll pull it out on the CV and send you the link tonight.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Alright. Yeah, send it. Worst case I learn something at the interview.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: That's exactly the right attitude. Same time next Tuesday, and well done this week Daniel, properly well done.

amara.label: AMARA × AISHA · DRIFTING · in-person re-engagement
amara.framing: Amara Adekoya, 34, returner after caring break. Childcare cost is real; CV anxiety. Aisha Khatun in person.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Amara, lovely to see you. Sit down. I'm Aisha, your re-engagement adviser. The system flagged that your text replies have dropped off this week. I'd rather get you in than guess what's going on.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Sorry. I know I've been quiet. The bus fare keeps adding up and I'm not sure I'm getting anywhere.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Right. Two things. I'm putting a thirty-pound travel voucher on your account today. That covers a fortnight, no questions, no paperwork.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Honestly that helps more than you think.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Second, your CV. You've rewritten it three times and lost your voice in it. We're going back to the original draft and I'll tidy it up myself. You sign it off. Twenty minutes, before you leave today.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: That actually sounds better than starting again.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Last thing. The four years caring for your mum isn't a gap. It is leadership and reliability and being the calm in a crisis. Employers will see it that way if we frame it that way.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Thanks Aisha. I was starting to think I was the problem.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: You are not the problem. The plan was a bit too busy. We're going to make it smaller and you and I will keep going.

wojciech.label: WOJCIECH × TOMASZ KOWALSKI · ON TRACK · in Polish
wojciech.framing: Wojciech, 52, Polish-speaking site supervisor, ON-TRACK. Paired with Tomasz Kowalski, Polish-speaking adviser at Harlesden. They speak in English on this call (translator nearby) but Tomasz can switch to Polish anytime.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Wojciech, this is Tomasz Kowalski calling. We can speak in Polish or English, whatever is easier. I am here for the supervisor interview prep.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Thank you Tomasz. English is fine for everyday, but for the supervisor interview I would like to be sure I am saying exactly what I mean.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Sensible. So, Brent Park Developments. Site Supervisor, twenty-eight days from now. They've shortlisted you. Twenty-eight years on UK sites and a Polish-English fluent supervisor is genuinely rare for them.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: I am ready for this. I want to ask them about safety procedures and the team they have currently. I will not just answer their questions, I will ask my own.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Good, that's exactly the move. One thing. Your CSCS Gold Card top-up has been approved this morning. Free to you. The certificate will arrive by Friday.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Excellent. With the Gold Card the salary band shifts up.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: It does. And travel costs for the interview are covered. The translator will join the interview as well, on the speaker, if you want one for that day. Many candidates say yes for the safety questions specifically.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Yes please. Let us add the translator. I would rather be precise than fast.

tomasz.label: TOMASZ × HANNAH · AT-RISK · same-day welfare call
tomasz.framing: Tomasz, 27, AT-RISK with mental health barriers. Hannah Brennan calls same-day. Warm, slow, no pressure.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Tomasz, it's Hannah. I know we weren't scheduled today but I wanted to hear your voice. Have you got a minute?

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Yeah. Sorry, I haven't been picking up much this week.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: It's alright. How are you actually doing, not the work bit, just generally?

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Not great if I'm honest. I'm not sleeping. The interview stuff has been spinning in my head and I keep thinking I'm going to mess it up and end up back where I was.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Thank you for telling me. That takes guts to say out loud, and none of what you just described means you're failing. It means you're carrying a lot.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Feels like I'm letting everyone down though.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: You are not letting anyone down. Here's what we do today. First, I'm pausing the job-match notifications for two weeks. No more new ones popping up. We're not adding pressure.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Okay, yeah, that would help.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Second, there's a Maximus service called Access to Work Mental Health Support. It's free, confidential, and a specialist will talk you through the sleep and anxiety side, separately from me. I'd like to refer you today, with your permission.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: It's free, yeah? I haven't got money for therapy.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Completely free, run by the same team I'm in. Nine months of support if you want it, you can stop whenever. Shall I make the call this afternoon?

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, do it. Thanks Hannah.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Last thing. I'm coming off the auto-plan and writing you a smaller one. Three things this week, not ten. We slow it down, we keep the door open, and we keep going. Alright?

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Alright. Thanks for ringing.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Always. I'll text you when the referral is in. Get some food and try to sleep tonight, Tomasz. We've got this.

fadumo.label: FADUMO × ELERI · DRIFTING · QTS pathway conversation
fadumo.framing: Fadumo, 31, Somali-speaking refugee, was a teacher in Mogadishu. Eleri (Welsh adviser) lays out the UK QTS path and short-term income.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Fadumo, it's Eleri. The translator is on the call. Today I want to talk through two paths for you. A teaching assistant role we can start next month, and the longer route to UK Qualified Teacher Status.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Thank you. I am ready. I worry that taking a teaching assistant job means I am stepping back from being a teacher.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: It's a fair worry. Let me be honest with you. UK QTS for an internationally trained teacher takes twelve to eighteen months. The teaching assistant role is paid work in a school, your foot in the door, and your hours count toward the QTS school experience requirement.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: So it is not a step back. It is the first step.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Exactly. Riverside Primary in Cardiff have shortlisted you. They know you were a primary teacher in Mogadishu. The head teacher specifically asked for your CV.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: That is very encouraging.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Now the practical side. The Adult Skills Fund will pay for your English for Academic Purposes course, which you'll need before the QTS application. No cost to you. The travel pass for school placement weeks is also covered.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: And for childcare for the youngest two during the days I am at school?

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: I asked the same. Restart has a childcare grant of forty pounds per child per day for the placement weeks. We will set that up this week. The translator will help you fill in the forms today if you would like.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Yes please. Today is good. Thank you Eleri.

mark.label: MARK × KAREN · ON TRACK · HGV reapplication and Ban-the-Box hire
mark.framing: Mark, 58, ex-offender, former HGV driver. Karen Mensah is direct, no hoops, lays out the Ban-the-Box pathway and HGV licence reapplication.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: Mark, it's Karen. Quick one for you. Bardon Aggregates have come back. They want you for the yard role with the HGV class C when your licence is reinstated. Twelve-month contract.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: They know about my conviction?

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: They do. Bardon is a Ban-the-Box employer. Your conviction was on the application. They've already taken the decision. The yard supervisor is named Ravi, he wants to meet you next Tuesday.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Right. And the licence?

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: DVLA have your reapplication. Class C medical is booked for Thursday at the Lincoln Centre. Restart pays for it. After the medical it's about six weeks for the licence to come back.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: So I take the yard work first, then I'm back on HGV when the licence is back. That's the deal.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: That's exactly the deal. The yard is forty hours, twenty-six thousand a year. When you move to HGV in six weeks the rate goes up to thirty-one. Bardon have agreed it in writing.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Good. Send me the appointment details for Tuesday and I will be there. None of the run-around.

lines.speaker: adviser
lines.text: No run-around. Email is in your inbox. One last thing. Universal Credit tapers off as your wages come in, not all at once. We'll do the better-off-in-work numbers with you before the Bardon start date. So you know.

lines.speaker: customer
lines.text: Sensible. Thanks Karen. I'll see you Tuesday.
